name: Maps and Scale — Shrinking the World to Fit
whatIsIt: A MAP is a small drawing of a big place, seen from above. A SCALE tells how map distance stands for real distance, e.g., '1 cm = 5 km'. To find a real distance, multiply the map length by the scale; to find a map length, divide the real distance by the scale.
whyNeeded: We can't carry a whole city, so we shrink it onto paper. Reading a scale lets us plan journeys, estimate how far places are, and understand plans of houses, parks and zoos — turning centimetres on paper into kilometres on the ground.
realLifeUses: A zoo or park map showing how far the tiger enclosure is
A road map: 1 cm on paper standing for many km of road
A house plan drawn to scale before it is built
Direction clues: north, south, east, west on the map

vocabulary.meaning: A small top-view drawing of a larger place.
The rule linking map distance to real distance, like 1 cm = 5 km.
The actual distance on the ground.
North, South, East, West — used to describe positions on a map.

story.title: The Village Map's Secret Scale
story.text: Ravi finds an old hand-drawn village map. 'The pond looks so close to the gate — only 3 cm!' Thatha points at the corner: 'Read the SCALE: 1 cm stands for 10 metres. So 3 cm means 3 × 10 = 30 real metres. The map shrinks the whole village to fit the paper — the scale is the secret to un-shrinking it.'
story.extractedProblem: On a map with scale 1 cm = 10 m, the pond is 3 cm from the gate. How far is it really?
story.answerInStory: 30 metres — 3 cm × 10 m per cm = 30 m.

visual.caption: Scale 1 cm = 10 m. A 3 cm map distance means 30 real metres.

teachingGallery.title: Scale — shrink the world to fit
teachingGallery.note: A scale like '1 cm = 5 km' means every 1 cm on the map is 5 km real.

examples.caption: Room plans use smaller scales, e.g. 1 cm = 2 m.

standardMethod.summary: Use the scale: real distance = map distance × scale; map distance = real distance ÷ scale.
steps: To get REAL distance: multiply the map centimetres by the scale number.
To get MAP distance: divide the real distance by the scale number.
Use directions (N, S, E, W) to describe which way.

alternateMethods.name: Unit-jump
steps: Count how many cm on the map.
Add that real distance up, one cm at a time, or multiply.
alternateMethods.example: 1 cm = 4 km, map shows 5 cm: 4×5 = 20 km.

alternateMethods.name: Direction-grid
steps: Say how far and in which direction (N/S/E/W).
Combine, e.g., '3 cm east then 2 cm north'.
alternateMethods.example: The school is 2 cm north of the market.

workedExamples.problem: A map has scale 1 cm = 5 km. Two towns are 4 cm apart on the map. How far apart are they really?
Scale 1 cm = 2 m. A room is really 8 m long. How long is it on the plan?
steps: To get MAP distance, divide by the scale.
It is 4 cm long on the plan.

trickPractice.intro: Real distance = map length × what one unit stands for.

questions.q: Scale: 1 cm = 5 km. The map shows 4 cm. Real distance?
Scale: 1 cm = 5 km. The map shows 3 cm. Real distance?
Scale: 1 cm = 10 km. The map shows 2 cm. Real distance?
Scale: 1 cm = 2 km. The map shows 6 cm. Real distance?
Scale: 1 cm = 5 km. The map shows 7 cm. Real distance?
Scale: 1 cm = 4 km. The map shows 5 cm. Real distance?

commonMistakes.mistake: Reading the map centimetres as the real distance, ignoring the scale.
commonMistakes.fix: The map is SHRUNK — always use the scale. 3 cm on a '1 cm = 10 m' map is 30 m, not 3 m.

commonMistakes.mistake: Dividing when you should multiply (or the reverse) between map and real distance.
commonMistakes.fix: Map → real: MULTIPLY by the scale (things get bigger). Real → map: DIVIDE by the scale (things get smaller to fit the paper).

commonMistakes.mistake: Getting the number but attaching the wrong unit (km instead of m).
commonMistakes.fix: Carry the scale's real-world unit through: if 1 cm = 10 m, the answer is in METRES.

easy.q: Scale: 1 cm = 10 m. A path is 2 cm on the map. How many metres is it really?
Scale: 1 cm = 5 km. Map shows 3 cm. Real distance in km?

medium.q: Scale: 1 cm = 4 km. Two towns are 6 cm apart on the map. Real km?
Scale: 1 cm = 2 m. A wall is really 10 m. How many cm on the plan?

challenge.q: Scale: 1 cm = 25 km. Map distance is 4 cm. Real km?
Scale: 1 cm = 3 m. A garden is really 21 m long. Length on the plan in cm?

questions.q: 1 cm = 10 m. Map 5 cm. Real metres?
1 cm = 6 km. Map 3 cm. Real km?
1 cm = 2 m. Real 12 m. Map cm?
1 cm = 5 m. Map 7 cm. Real metres?
1 cm = 4 km. Real 20 km. Map cm?

teachBackPrompt: Draw a simple map of your room to a scale like '1 cm = 1 metre'. Teach someone at home how to turn a map length into the real length using the scale — and why you multiply going map-to-real.

revisionCard.summary: A map shrinks a big place; the SCALE un-shrinks it. Map → real: MULTIPLY by the scale. Real → map: DIVIDE. Keep the scale's real unit (m or km).

gameMission.title: The Treasure Map's Secret
gameMission.brief: Read scales to turn tiny map distances into real ones and guide the hunt across the island.

realLifeProject: Find a map (bus route, mall directory, or atlas). Read its scale and work out the real distance between two places on it.
